div1.entryFree: Carouse, vb., to drink hard: having all day --d and banqueted, H6A II, 1, 12. we were --ing to the second cock, Mcb. II, 3, 26. c. together like friends long lost, Ant. IV, 12, 12. Transitively: that blood . . . hast thou tapped out and drunkenly --d, R2 II, 1, 127. cf. Oth. II, 3, 55. Followed by to, == to drink, to empty a cup to the health of a person, to toast: as if he had been aboard, --ing to his mates, Shr. III, 2, 173. c. full measure to her maidenhead, Shr. III, 2, 173 the queen --s to thy fortune, Hml. V, 2, 300. Roderigo . . . . to Desdemona hath to-night --d potations pottle-deep, Oth. II, 3, 55.
